skill.name: docusaurus-deployer
skill.description: Deploy Docusaurus documentation sites to GitHub Pages with local validation, GitHub Actions setup, and troubleshooting guidance.
skill.summary: Guides Docusaurus builds, validation, and GitHub Pages deployment through GitHub Actions.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, URL, filesystem, and token patterns, but manual review found documentation and workflow templates rather than malicious executable code. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it instructs agents to run npm, git, GitHub CLI, and cleanup commands in user repositories and to configure GitHub Pages permissions.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Executed Deployment Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run npm build commands and git commit or push commands in the user repository. This is legitimate for a deployment skill, but it can modify project state, publish changes, or execute package scripts from the target project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and are meant to be executed by the agent. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands match the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: GitHub Pages Permission and Secret Handling
medium_findings.description: The bundled workflow and guide require Pages write and id-token permissions and describe use of GitHub secrets. These settings are normal for GitHub Pages deployment, but misuse can expose deployment credentials or grant unnecessary publishing access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission and secret references are present and semantically relevant. The workflow uses standard GitHub Pages permissions, so this is a controlled but review-worthy risk.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Cleanup Troubleshooting Command
medium_findings.description: Troubleshooting guidance includes removing node_modules and package-lock.json before reinstalling dependencies. This can alter dependency resolution and should only be run after user confirmation and version-control review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cleanup command is clearly present and deletes local files. It is a common repair step, but removing the lockfile changes reproducibility.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts refer to GitHub Pages examples, localhost validation, and official documentation links. Manual review found no evidence that the skill sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are examples or reference links in Markdown. No network client code or exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography alerts point to Markdown deployment text and examples, not cryptographic APIs or hashing logic. No evidence found of weak cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found deployment and documentation guidance only. There is no code path that performs cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: Path traversal and reconnaissance alerts come from relative-link examples, file checks, and diagnostic commands. These are normal documentation validation steps, not attempts to escape directories or inventory a host secretly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is plainly presented as Markdown link syntax or troubleshooting checks. The commands may reveal local state, but they are user-visible diagnostics.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell and Package Manager Commands in Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for npm, git, GitHub CLI, and local process checks. Agents should request confirmation before running commands that modify files, install packages, or push commits.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are numerous and actionable. They are expected for this skill but still create side-effect risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workflow Permissions for Deployment
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub Actions template grants Pages write and id-token write permissions. This is appropriate for GitHub Pages, but marketplace users should review repository permissions before enabling it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow permission block is explicit and deployment related. It is not malicious, but it can publish repository content to GitHub Pages.

content.value_statement: Docusaurus deployments can fail because configuration, build output, and GitHub Pages settings must align. This skill guides local validation, workflow setup, and deployment checks before publishing.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Docusaurus configuration for GitHub Pages URL and baseUrl settings.
Guides local validation with dependency installation, type checking, build, and serve commands.
Provides a GitHub Actions workflow template for Pages deployment.
Explains repository Pages settings, branch protection, and deployment monitoring.
Troubleshoots common build, permission, 404, MDX, and workflow failures.
Defines performance, accessibility, and content quality checks before publishing.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a hosted deployment service.
Commands run against the user repository and require user review before execution.
Workflow paths may need changes when the Docusaurus app is not in the documented directory.
It does not verify live GitHub settings unless the agent has appropriate repository access.

use_cases.title: Launch Documentation Site
use_cases.description: Configure a Docusaurus project for GitHub Pages and publish it through GitHub Actions.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Release Docs
use_cases.description: Add local validation and CI checks before documentation changes reach production.

use_cases.title: Fix Failed Pages Deployment
use_cases.description: Diagnose broken links, 404 errors, workflow permissions, and build failures after publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Docusaurus Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Docusaurus project for GitHub Pages readiness. Check docusaurus.config, sidebars, package settings, and required deployment fields.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare GitHub Pages Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up this Docusaurus site for GitHub Pages. Update the config, explain required repository settings, and identify commands I should approve before running.

prompt_templates.title: Create Deployment Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions deployment plan for my Docusaurus site. Use the correct working directory, Node version, build steps, artifact path, and Pages permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Production Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this failed Docusaurus GitHub Pages deployment. Compare local build results, workflow logs, Pages settings, baseUrl, artifacts, and permission configuration.

output_examples.input: A project repository has a Docusaurus config and no deployment workflow.
output: A readiness checklist covering URL, baseUrl, organization, project name, and Node version.
A local validation sequence for install, type check, build, and serve steps.
A workflow setup summary with Pages permissions and artifact path notes.

output_examples.input: A GitHub Pages site returns 404 after a successful workflow run.
output: A diagnosis plan that checks baseUrl, Pages source, deployment history, and build artifacts.
A likely-cause summary ranked by configuration risk.
A short list of repository settings to confirm before rerunning the workflow.

output_examples.input: A documentation build fails because of MDX or TypeScript errors.
output: A local troubleshooting checklist for type checking, build logs, imports, and MDX syntax.
Guidance to fix errors before committing deployment changes.
A final pre-push quality gate for links, assets, and accessibility checks.

best_practices: Run local validation before committing workflow or configuration changes.
Review every command that changes files, installs dependencies, or pushes to main.
Confirm GitHub Pages source, permissions, and baseUrl before triggering deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not push deployment changes before the local build and type checks pass.
Do not copy workflow paths without matching them to the actual project directory.
Do not expose custom secrets or broaden workflow permissions beyond Pages deployment needs.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy the site automatically?
faq.answer: It guides the agent through deployment steps. Users should approve commands that change files, install packages, or push commits.

faq.question: Which hosting target does it support?
faq.answer: It focuses on Docusaurus deployments to GitHub Pages using GitHub Actions.

faq.question: Can it handle project Pages and user Pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains the different url, baseUrl, projectName, and deployment branch settings for both targets.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it includes shell, git, package manager, and workflow permission steps with real side effects.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub secrets?
faq.answer: The standard Pages workflow can use the built-in GitHub token. Any additional secrets should be reviewed before use.

faq.question: Can it fix broken deployments?
faq.answer: It provides troubleshooting paths for build failures, 404 errors, permission issues, broken links, and stale deployments.
